name: @fuzdev/tsv-benches
description: tsv benchmark harness deps — the single source of truth for the npm package versions the bench measures against. Consumed by BOTH runtimes: Deno reads it via `nodeModulesDir: "manual"` in deno.json, Node reads it directly. Install with `npm install` (the one installer). The jsr-only `@std/*` packages, if any, stay in deno.json.
//canonical-sync: ⚠ The 5 canonical packages (prettier, svelte, acorn, @sveltejs/acorn-typescript, prettier-plugin-svelte) are ALSO pinned as literals in crates/tsv_debug/src/deno/sidecar.ts and MUST match — agreement (and ../svelte / ../acorn-typescript checkout alignment, when present) is enforced by `deno task pins:audit` (scripts/check_canonical_pins.ts, in `deno task check`). Bumping any re-baselines the whole fixture corpus — see benches/js/CLAUDE.md §'Canonical baseline is coupled'. The other deps (oxc, oxfmt, biome, etc.) are free to bump independently.
//engines: Node ≥ 22.18 is the native TS type-stripping floor — the harness entries (bench.ts, install_deps.ts) run as TypeScript directly under `node`. Advisory (npm warns without engine-strict); an older Node fails with an opaque parse error before any runtime guard could fire.
//types: @types/node is types-only and dev-only (never ships, no runtime cost). It lets the shared harness's `node:` builtins type-check under `deno check` (and editors) when an npm package's .d.ts pulls in node types in npm-resolution mode — the portable-node:-builtins architecture's one new dev dep. NOT needed by the gated `deno task test:deno` (that's the dependency-free divergence suite — `node:assert` + relative imports, no node_modules).
//oxc-wasi: The oxc-parser WASM bench row needs @oxc-parser/binding-wasm32-wasi — pure-wasm (runtime-portable) but its metadata declares `cpu: wasm32`, so npm skips it on any non-wasm host. It is NOT listed as a dep here: declaring it errors on x64, and as an optionalDependency npm records it as known-skipped so a later `--force` reinstall no-ops. Instead `deno task bench:install` (install_deps.ts) force-fetches it at the oxc-parser version (oxc ships all bindings in lockstep), so the `oxc-parser` pin above is its single source of truth too.
